SwarmBrain.Protocol.doc: The Compressor.   Converts high-level thoughts (Structs) into low-level telegrams (Bytes).   Protocol Format (14 Bytes Total):   [HEADER(1)][ID(4)][LAT(4)][LON(4)][CLASS(1)]...

SwarmBrain.Antenna.doc: The Receiver.   It listens for incoming thoughts from other nodes and passes them to the Pipeline....

SwarmBrain.Pipeline.doc: The Main Circuit Board.   Connects Inputs -> Cortex -> Persistence.   Now features "Dual-Mode Switching" (WiFi vs Radio)....

SwarmBrain.Radio.doc: The Wilderness Link.   Talks directly to the EMAX 2W module via UART.   No TCP. No Handshakes. Just raw metal....

SwarmBrain.Eye.doc: The Input Stage.   Responsible for hardware interaction and signal conditioning....

SwarmBrain.Discovery.doc: The Civilian Link.   Manages WiFi/Erlang Distribution connections.   If this fails, the Pipeline automatically falls back to Radio....

SwarmBrain.Observation.doc: The Standard Protocol.   This struct is the "Signal" that flows through the Class A Amplifier.   Includes logic for "Heavy Packet" optimization (Payload Pruning)....

SwarmBrain.Persistence.doc: The Long-Term Memory.   Implements "Genesis Boot" to handle Split-Brain scenarios automatically.   Includes "Ghost in the Shell" Black Box writing for crash resilience....

import os
import json
import fnmatch
import re
import hashlib

# --- CONFIGURATION ---
MAX_FILE_SIZE = 1024 * 1024  
IGNORE_DIRS = [
    '.git', '__pycache__', 'deps', '_build', 'node_modules', 
    '.elixir_ls', '.idea', '.vscode',
    'Mnesia.*', 'mnesia.*', 'priv', 'assets'
]
IGNORE_EXTENSIONS = [
    '.beam', '.ez', '.dll', '.so', '.o', '.a',
    '.DCD', '.DAT', '.LOG', '.dcd', '.dat', '.log',
    '.jpg', '.jpeg', '.png', '.gif', '.mp3', '.wav',
    '.zip', '.tar', '.gz', '.lock'
]

# --- THE X-RAY SCANNER (v7.0 - TYPES & CONFIG) ---

def extract_config_skeleton(content):
    """
    Scans config/*.exs files to understand the Runtime Environment.
    Captures: config :app, :key, value
    """
    configs = []
    # Matches: config :swarm_brain, :cortex_module, SwarmBrain.Cortex.ResNet
    matches = re.findall(r'^\s*config\s+:([a-zA-Z0-9_]+),\s*:([a-zA-Z0-9_]+),\s*(.+)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    for app, key, val in matches:
        configs.append(f":{app} | :{key} => {val.strip()}")
    return configs

def extract_elixir_skeleton(content):
    skeleton = {
        "doc": None,        # The Intent (@moduledoc)
        "modules": [],
        "dependencies": [], 
        "behaviors": [],   
        "structs": [],     
        "functions": [],    
        "types": [],        # The Constraints (@type, @spec)
        "attributes": [],   
        "todos": []         
    }
    
    # 0. The Intent (Module Doc)
    # Grabs the first 200 chars of the moduledoc to understand "Why"
    doc_match = re.search(r'@moduledoc\s+"""(.*?)"""', content, re.DOTALL)
    if doc_match:
        skeleton["doc"] = doc_match.group(1).strip()[:200].replace("\n", " ") + "..."

    # 1. Modules
    modules = re.findall(r'defmodule\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\._]*)', content)
    skeleton["modules"] = modules
    
    # 2. Dependencies
    aliases = re.findall(r'^\s*alias\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    skeleton["dependencies"].extend([f"alias {a}" for a in aliases])
    
    imports = re.findall(r'^\s*import\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    skeleton["dependencies"].extend([f"import {i}" for i in imports])

    # 3. Behaviors
    uses = re.findall(r'^\s*use\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    skeleton["behaviors"].extend([f"use {u}" for u in uses])
    
    behaviours = re.findall(r'^\s*@behaviour\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    skeleton["behaviors"].extend([f"implements {b}" for b in behaviours])

    # 4. Data Shapes
    struct_match = re.search(r'defstruct\s+\[(.*?)\]', content, re.DOTALL)
    if struct_match:
        raw_keys = struct_match.group(1)
        keys = re.findall(r':([a-z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)', raw_keys)
        skeleton["structs"] = keys

    # 5. Functions & 5b. Types (EVOLUTIONARY IMPROVEMENT)
    
    # Extract Specs (Contracts)
    specs = re.findall(r'^\s*@spec\s+(.+)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    skeleton["types"].extend([f"@spec {s.strip()}" for s in specs])

    # Extract Functions
    def_matches = re.finditer(r'^\s*(def|defp)\s+([a-z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*[\?!]?)\s*(\(.*\))?\s*(do|,)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    for m in def_matches:
        kind = m.group(1)
        name = m.group(2)
        args_str = m.group(3)
        arity = 0
        if args_str:
            arity = args_str.count(',') + 1
        skeleton["functions"].append(f"{kind} {name}/{arity}")

    # 6. Attributes
    attrs = re.findall(r'^\s*(@[a-z_]+)\s+(.+)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    skeleton["attributes"] = [f"{k} = {v}" for k, v in attrs if k not in ['@moduledoc', '@doc', '@behaviour', '@spec', '@type']]

    # 7. The Debt Collector
    todos = re.findall(r'#\s*(TODO|FIXME|HACK|NOTE):?\s*(.*)', content, re.IGNORECASE)
    skeleton["todos"] = [f"{tag.upper()}: {msg.strip()}" for tag, msg in todos]

    return skeleton

def generate_tree(path, prefix=""):
    tree_str = ""
    try:
        items = os.listdir(path)
    except PermissionError:
        return ""
    items.sort(key=lambda x: (not os.path.isdir(os.path.join(path, x)), x))
    filtered_items = []
    for item in items:
        if not any(fnmatch.fnmatch(item, pattern) for pattern in IGNORE_DIRS):
            filtered_items.append(item)
    pointers = [("├── " if i < len(filtered_items) - 1 else "└── ") for i in range(len(filtered_items))]
    for pointer, item in zip(pointers, filtered_items):
        full_path = os.path.join(path, item)
        tree_str += prefix + pointer + item + "\n"
        if os.path.isdir(full_path):
            extension = "│   " if pointer == "├── " else "    "
            tree_str += generate_tree(full_path, prefix + extension)
    return tree_str

def is_binary(file_path):
    try:
        with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:
            chunk = f.read(1024)
            if b'\0' in chunk:
                return True
            try:
                chunk.decode('utf-8')
            except UnicodeDecodeError:
                return True
    except Exception:
        return True
    return False

def get_file_hash(file_path):
    h = hashlib.md5()
    try:
        with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:
            for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(4096), b""):
                h.update(chunk)
        return h.hexdigest()[:8]
    except:
        return "error"

def pack_repo(repo_path, output_file):
    system_map = {} 
    runtime_config = [] # <--- REVOLUTIONARY: The Config Store

    repo_data = {
        "project_name": os.path.basename(os.path.abspath(repo_path)),
        "tree_view": generate_tree(repo_path),
        "system_map": {}, 
        "runtime_config": [], # <--- Exposed to the Void
        "files": []
    }

    print(f"💀 X-Raying Architecture: {repo_path}")

    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(repo_path):
        for i in range(len(dirs) - 1, -1, -1):
            d = dirs[i]
            if any(fnmatch.fnmatch(d, pattern) for pattern in IGNORE_DIRS):
                dirs.pop(i)

        for file in files:
            file_path = os.path.join(root, file)
            rel_path = os.path.relpath(file_path, repo_path)
            
            _, ext = os.path.splitext(file)
            if ext in IGNORE_EXTENSIONS:
                continue

            file_size = os.path.getsize(file_path)
            file_type = "binary" if is_binary(file_path) else "text"
            
            if file_type == "text":
                try:
                    with open(file_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore') as f:
                        full_content = f.read() 
                        
                        # Extract Skeleton
                        skeleton = None
                        if ext in ['.ex', '.exs']:
                            skeleton = extract_elixir_skeleton(full_content)
                            
                            if skeleton["modules"]:
                                mod_name = skeleton["modules"][0]
                                system_map[mod_name] = {
                                    "doc": skeleton["doc"], # NOW WE KNOW "WHY"
                                    "functions": skeleton["functions"],
                                    "types": skeleton["types"], # NOW WE KNOW "HOW"
                                    "dependencies": skeleton["dependencies"]
                                }
                                
                            # REVOLUTIONARY: Config Parsing
                            if "config" in rel_path and ext == ".exs":
                                config_lines = extract_config_skeleton(full_content)
                                runtime_config.extend(config_lines)

                        # Content Truncation Logic
                        if file_size > MAX_FILE_SIZE:
                            head = full_content[:2000]
                            display_content = head + f"\n\n[... ✂️ VOID CUT: File too large ({file_size} bytes). Skeleton extracted below. ...]"
                            print(f"⚠️ Truncating content for: {rel_path}")
                        else:
                            display_content = full_content
                        
                        file_entry = {
                            "path": rel_path,
                            "content": display_content
                        }

                        if skeleton:
                            file_entry["skeleton"] = skeleton
                        
                        repo_data["files"].append(file_entry)

                except Exception as e:
                    print(f"❌ Error: {rel_path} - {e}")
            else:
                repo_data["files"].append({
                    "path": rel_path,
                    "size": file_size,
                    "type": "binary",
                    "hash": get_file_hash(file_path)
                })

    # Inject Maps
    repo_data["system_map"] = system_map
    repo_data["runtime_config"] = runtime_config

    print(f"💾 Compressing into the Void: {output_file}")
    with open(output_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        json.dump(repo_data, f, indent=2)
    print("✅ Analysis Complete.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    pack_repo(".", "swarm_archive.json")

defmodule SwarmBrain.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :swarm_brain,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.15",
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      deps: deps(),
      # This ensures we don't accidentally compile inside the SSD if you didn't set the env vars
      build_path: System.get_env("MIX_BUILD_PATH") || "_build",
      deps_path: System.get_env("MIX_DEPS_PATH") || "deps"
    ]
  end

  # Run "mix help compile.app" to learn about applications.
  def application do
    [
      extra_applications: [:logger, :runtime_tools, :os_mon, :mnesia],
      mod: {SwarmBrain.Application, []}
    ]
  end

  # Run "mix help deps" to learn about dependencies.
  defp deps do
    [
      # --- 🧠 Neural Network Core ---
      {:bumblebee, "~> 0.6.0"},    # Pre-trained models (YOLOv8, ResNet, etc.)
      {:axon, "~> 0.7.0"},         # Neural Network framework
      {:nx, "~> 0.9.0"},           # Numerical Elixir (Tensors)
      {:circuits_uart, "~> 1.5"},

      # --- 🚀 Acceleration (The Engine) ---
      # EXLA is the compiler that makes Elixir fast for Math.
      # If you have NVIDIA: It uses CUDA.
      # If you have Intel/No GPU: It uses the CPU (still fast!).
      {:exla, "~> 0.9.0"},

      # --- 👁️ Vision & Utilities ---
      {:stb_image, "~> 0.6.0"},    # For decoding JPEGs from the DSLR
      {:req, "~> 0.5.0"},          # HTTP client (needed to download the YOLO model)
      {:kino, "~> 0.14.0"},        # Optional: Great for debugging visuals in Livebook

      # --- 🐝 Swarm & Distribution ---
      {:libcluster, "~> 3.4"},     # Automatic node discovery
      {:horde, "~> 0.9.0"},        # Distributed supervision
      {:delta_crdt, "~> 0.6.5"},   # Conflict-free Replicated Data Types (The Shared Brain)

      # --- 🚁 Flight Control ---
      # (Optional) If this node sends MAVLink commands directly
      # {:mave, "~> 0.1.0"},       # Or your preferred MAVLink library
    ]
  end
end

#!/usr/bin/env bash


# 🕵️ Auto-detect the local LAN IP
# This grabs the first non-loopback IP address. 
# If it guesses wrong on the HP, just hardcode IP="192.168.1.147"
IP=$(ifconfig | grep -Eo 'inet (addr:)?([0-9]*\.){3}[0-9]*' | grep -Eo '([0-9]*\.){3}[0-9]*' | grep -v '127.0.0.1' | head -n1)

# If ifconfig is missing in NixOS minimal, use 'ip':
if [ -z "$IP" ]; then
    IP=$(ip addr show | grep -oP '(?<=inet\s)\d+(\.\d+){3}' | grep -v '127.0.0.1' | head -n1)
fi

NAME="brain@$IP"
COOKIE="swarm_class_a_secret"

echo "🚀 IGNITING SWARM NODE: $NAME"
echo "   - 🍪 Cookie: Class A Secret"
echo "   - 🛣️  Ports: 40000-40100 (Firewall Lane)"

# The Magic Command
iex --name "$NAME" \
    --cookie "$COOKIE" \
    --erl "-kernel inet_dist_listen_min 40000 inet_dist_listen_max 40100" \
    -S mix

      "content": "import os
import json
import fnmatch
import re
import hashlib

# --- CONFIGURATION ---
MAX_FILE_SIZE = 1024 * 1024  
IGNORE_DIRS = [
    '.git', '__pycache__', 'deps', '_build', 'node_modules', 
    '.elixir_ls', '.idea', '.vscode',
    'Mnesia.*', 'mnesia.*', 'priv', 'assets'
]
IGNORE_EXTENSIONS = [
    '.beam', '.ez', '.dll', '.so', '.o', '.a',
    '.DCD', '.DAT', '.LOG', '.dcd', '.dat', '.log',
    '.jpg', '.jpeg', '.png', '.gif', '.mp3', '.wav',
    '.zip', '.tar', '.gz', '.lock'
]

# --- THE X-RAY SCANNER (v6.0 - THE GLOBAL MAP) ---
def extract_elixir_skeleton(content):
    skeleton = {
        "modules": [],
        "dependencies": [], 
        "behaviors": [],   
        "structs": [],     
        "functions": [],    # Renamed from public_functions to include defp
        "attributes": [],   # NEW: Constants
        "todos": []         
    }
    
    # 1. Modules
    modules = re.findall(r'defmodule\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\._]*)', content)
    skeleton["modules"] = modules
    
    # 2. Dependencies
    aliases = re.findall(r'^\s*alias\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    skeleton["dependencies"].extend([f"alias {a}" for a in aliases])
    
    imports = re.findall(r'^\s*import\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    skeleton["dependencies"].extend([f"import {i}" for i in imports])

    # 3. Behaviors
    uses = re.findall(r'^\s*use\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    skeleton["behaviors"].extend([f"use {u}" for u in uses])
    
    behaviours = re.findall(r'^\s*@behaviour\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    skeleton["behaviors"].extend([f"implements {b}" for b in behaviours])

    # 4. Data Shapes
    struct_match = re.search(r'defstruct\s+\[(.*?)\]', content, re.DOTALL)
    if struct_match:
        raw_keys = struct_match.group(1)
        keys = re.findall(r':([a-z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)', raw_keys)
        skeleton["structs"] = keys

    # 5. Functions (Public AND Private) - EVOLUTIONARY IMPROVEMENT
    def_matches = re.finditer(r'^\s*(def|defp)\s+([a-z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*[\?!]?)\s*(\(.*\))?\s*(do|,)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    for m in def_matches:
        kind = m.group(1)
        name = m.group(2)
        args_str = m.group(3)
        arity = 0
        if args_str:
            arity = args_str.count(',') + 1
        skeleton["functions"].append(f"{kind} {name}/{arity}")

    # 6. Attributes (Constants) - EVOLUTIONARY IMPROVEMENT
    attrs = re.findall(r'^\s*(@[a-z_]+)\s+(.+)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    skeleton["attributes"] = [f"{k} = {v}" for k, v in attrs if k not in ['@moduledoc', '@doc', '@behaviour']]

    # 7. The Debt Collector
    todos = re.findall(r'#\s*(TODO|FIXME|HACK|NOTE):?\s*(.*)', content, re.IGNORECASE)
    skeleton["todos"] = [f"{tag.upper()}: {msg.strip()}" for tag, msg in todos]

    return skeleton

def generate_tree(path, prefix=""):
    tree_str = ""
    try:
        items = os.listdir(path)
    except PermissionError:
        return ""
    items.sort(key=lambda x: (not os.path.isdir(os.path.join(path, x)), x))
    filtered_items = []
    for item in items:
        if not any(fnmatch.fnmatch(item, pattern) for pattern in IGNORE_DIRS):
            filtered_items.append(item)
    pointers = [("├── " if i < len(filtered_items) - 1 else "└── ") for i in range(len(filtered_items))]
    for pointer, item in zip(pointers, filtered_items):
        full_path = os.path.join(path, item)
        tree_str += prefix + pointer + item + "\n"
        if os.path.isdir(full_path):
            extension = "│   " if pointer == "├── " else "    "
            tree_str += generate_tree(full_path, prefix + extension)
    return tree_str

def is_binary(file_path):
    try:
        with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:
            chunk = f.read(1024)
            if b'\0' in chunk:
                return True
            try:
                chunk.decode('utf-8')
            except UnicodeDecodeError:
                return True
    except Exception:
        return True
    return False

def get_file_hash(file_path):
    h = hashlib.md5()
    try:
        with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:
            for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(4096), b""):
                h.update(chunk)
        return h.hexdigest()[:8]
    except:
        return "error"

def pack_repo(repo_path, output_file):
    # REVOLUTIONARY CHANGE: Pre-initialize the System Map
    system_map = {} 

    repo_data = {
        "project_name": os.path.basename(os.path.abspath(repo_path)),
        "tree_view": generate_tree(repo_path),
        "system_map": {}, # <--- The Global Cortex Header
        "files": []
    }

    print(f"💀 X-Raying Architecture: {repo_path}")

    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(repo_path):
        for i in range(len(dirs) - 1, -1, -1):
            d = dirs[i]
            if any(fnmatch.fnmatch(d, pattern) for pattern in IGNORE_DIRS):
                dirs.pop(i)

        for file in files:
            file_path = os.path.join(root, file)
            rel_path = os.path.relpath(file_path, repo_path)
            
            _, ext = os.path.splitext(file)
            if ext in IGNORE_EXTENSIONS:
                continue

            file_size = os.path.getsize(file_path)
            file_type = "binary" if is_binary(file_path) else "text"
            
            if file_type == "text":
                try:
                    with open(file_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore') as f:
                        full_content = f.read() 
                        
                        # Extract Skeleton
                        skeleton = None
                        if ext in ['.ex', '.exs']:
                            skeleton = extract_elixir_skeleton(full_content)
                            
                            # REVOLUTIONARY: Add to Global System Map
                            # We use the path as the key, but we strip the Skeleton down to just modules/functions
                            # for the high-level map to save space.
                            if skeleton["modules"]:
                                mod_name = skeleton["modules"][0]
                                system_map[mod_name] = {
                                    "functions": skeleton["functions"],
                                    "dependencies": skeleton["dependencies"]
                                }

                        # Content Truncation Logic
                        if file_size > MAX_FILE_SIZE:
                            head = full_content[:2000]
                            display_content = head + f"\n\n[... ✂️ VOID CUT: File too large ({file_size} bytes). Skeleton extracted below. ...]"
                            print(f"⚠️ Truncating content for: {rel_path}")
                        else:
                            display_content = full_content
                        
                        file_entry = {
                            "path": rel_path,
                            "content": display_content
                        }

                        if skeleton:
                            file_entry["skeleton"] = skeleton
                        
                        repo_data["files"].append(file_entry)

                except Exception as e:
                    print(f"❌ Error: {rel_path} - {e}")
            else:
                # Binary files (just metadata)
                repo_data["files"].append({
                    "path": rel_path,
                    "size": file_size,
                    "type": "binary",
                    "hash": get_file_hash(file_path)
                })

    # Inject the Global Map at the top level
    repo_data["system_map"] = system_map

    print(f"💾 Compressing into the Void: {output_file}")
    with open(output_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        json.dump(repo_data, f, indent=2)
    print("✅ Analysis Complete.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    pack_repo(".", "swarm_archive.json")"

THE 1MB LIMIT (Expanded Aperture)\nMAX_FILE_SIZE = 1024 * 1024  \nIGNORE_DIRS = [\n    '.git', '__pycache__', 'deps', '_build', 'node_modules', \n    '.elixir_ls', '.idea', '.vscode',\n    'Mnesia.*', 'mnesia.*', 'priv', 'assets'\n]\nIGNORE_EXTENSIONS = [\n    '.beam', '.ez', '.dll', '.so', '.o', '.a',\n    '.DCD', '.DAT', '.LOG', '.dcd', '.dat', '.log',\n    '.jpg', '.jpeg', '.png', '.gif', '.mp3', '.wav',\n    '.zip', '.tar', '.gz', '.lock'\n]\n\n# --- THE X-RAY SCANNER (v5.0 - DEBT COLLECTOR) ---\ndef extract_elixir_skeleton(content):\n    \"\"\"\n    Extracts not just functions, but CONNECTIONS (aliases), DATA SHAPES (structs),\n    and TECHNICAL DEBT (Todos).\n    \"\"\"\n    skeleton = {\n        \"modules\": [],\n        \"dependencies\": [], # alias, import, require\n        \"behaviors\": [],    # use, @behaviour\n        \"structs\": [],      # defstruct keys\n        \"public_functions\": [],\n        \"todos\": []         # The Debt Collector\n    }\n    \n    # 1. Modules\n    modules = re.findall(r'defmodule\\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\\._]*)', content)\n    skeleton[\"modules\"] = modules\n    \n    # 2. Dependencies (The Tendons)\n    aliases = re.findall(r'^\\s*alias\\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)\n    skeleton[\"dependencies\"].extend([f\"alias {a}\" for a in aliases])\n    \n    imports = re.findall(r'^\\s*import\\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)\n    skeleton[\"dependencies\"].extend([f\"import {i}\" for i in imports])\n\n    # 3. Behaviors (The Contracts)\n    uses = re.findall(r'^\\s*use\\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)\n    skeleton[\"behaviors\"].extend([f\"use {u}\" for u in uses])\n    \n    behaviours = re.findall(r'^\\s*@behaviour\\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)\n    skeleton[\"behaviors\"].extend([f\"implements {b}\" for b in behaviours])\n\n    # 4. Data Shapes (The Bones)\n    struct_match = re.search(r'defstruct\\s+\\[(.*?)\\]', content, re.DOTALL)\n    if struct_match:\n        raw_keys = struct_match.group(1)\n        keys = re.findall(r':([a-z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)', raw_keys)\n        skeleton[\"structs\"] = keys\n\n    # 5. Public Functions with Arity (The Muscles)\n    def_matches = re.finditer(r'^\\s*def\\s+([a-z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*[\\?!]?)\\s*(\\(.*\\))?\\s*(do|,)', content, re.MULTILINE)\n    for m in def_matches:\n        name = m.group(1)\n        args_str = m.group(2)\n        arity = 0\n        if args_str:\n            arity = args_str.count(',') + 1\n        skeleton[\"public_functions\"].append(f\"{name}/{arity}\")\n\n    # 6. The Debt Collector (New)\n    # Finds \"# TODO: fix this\" or \"# FIXME: memory leak\"\n    todos = re.findall(r'#\\s*(TODO|FIXME|HACK|NOTE):?\\s*(.*)', content, re.IGNORECASE)\n    # Normalize to uppercase tag for clean JSON\n    skeleton[\"todos\"] = [f\"{tag.upper()}: {msg.strip()}\" for tag, msg in todos]\n\n    return skeleton\n\ndef generate_tree(path, prefix=\"\"):\n    tree_str = \"\"\n    try:\n        items = os.listdir(path)\n    except PermissionError:\n        return \"\"\n    \n    items.sort(key=lambda x: (not os.path.isdir(os.path.join(path, x)), x))\n    \n    filtered_items = []\n    for item in items:\n        if not any(fnmatch.fnmatch(item, pattern) for pattern in IGNORE_DIRS):\n            filtered_items.append(item)\n    \n    pointers = [(\"\u251c\u2500\u2500 \" if i < len(filtered_items) - 1 else \"\u2514\u2500\u2500 \") for i in range(len(filtered_items))]\n\n    for pointer, item in zip(pointers, filtered_items):\n        full_path = os.path.join(path, item)\n        tree_str += prefix + pointer + item + \"\\n\"\n        \n        if os.path.isdir(full_path):\n            extension = \"\u2502   \" if pointer == \"\u251c\u2500\u2500 \" else \"    \"\n            tree_str += generate_tree(full_path, prefix + extension)\n            \n    return tree_str\n\ndef is_binary(file_path):\n    try:\n        with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:\n            chunk = f.read(1024)\n            if b'\\0' in chunk:\n                return True\n            try:\n                chunk.decode('utf-8')\n            except UnicodeDecodeError:\n                return True\n    except Exception:\n        return True\n    return False\n\ndef get_file_hash(file_path):\n    h = hashlib.md5()\n    try:\n        with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:\n            for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(4096), b\"\"):\n                h.update(chunk)\n        return h.hexdigest()[:8]\n    except:\n        return \"error\"\n\ndef pack_repo(repo_path, output_file):\n    repo_data = {\n        \"project_name\": os.path.basename(os.path.abspath(repo_path)),\n        \"tree_view\": generate_tree(repo_path),\n        \"structure\": [],\n        \"files\": []\n    }\n\n    print(f\"\ud83d\udc80 X-Raying Architecture: {repo_path}\")\n\n    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(repo_path):\n        for i in range(len(dirs) - 1, -1, -1):\n            d = dirs[i]\n            if any(fnmatch.fnmatch(d, pattern) for pattern in IGNORE_DIRS):\n                dirs.pop(i)\n\n        for file in files:\n            file_path = os.path.join(root, file)\n            rel_path = os.path.relpath(file_path, repo_path)\n            \n            _, ext = os.path.splitext(file)\n            if ext in IGNORE_EXTENSIONS:\n                continue\n\n            file_size = os.path.getsize(file_path)\n            file_type = \"binary\" if is_binary(file_path) else \"text\"\n            \n            file_meta = {\n                \"path\": rel_path,\n                \"size\": file_size,\n                \"type\": file_type,\n                \"hash\": get_file_hash(file_path)\n            }\n            repo_data[\"structure\"].append(file_meta)\n\n            if file_type == \"text\":\n                try:\n                    with open(file_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore') as f:\n                        # 1. READ EVERYTHING FIRST (The Fix)\n                        full_content = f.read() \n                        \n                        # 2. Extract Skeleton from the FULL body\n                        skeleton = None\n                        if ext in ['.ex', '.exs']:\n                            skeleton = extract_elixir_skeleton(full_content)\n\n                        # 3. NOW decide what to show in the \"content\" field\n                        if file_size > MAX_FILE_SIZE:\n                            head = full_content[:2000] # Give a bit more context since we bumped limits\n                            display_content = head + f\"\\n\\n[... \u2702\ufe0f VOID CUT: File too large ({file_size} bytes). Skeleton extracted below. ...]\"\n                            print(f\"\u26a0\ufe0f Truncating content for: {rel_path} (Skeleton preserved)\")\n                        else:\n                            display_content = full_content\n                        \n                        file_entry = {\n                            \"path\": rel_path,\n                            \"content\": display_content\n                        }\n\n                        if skeleton:\n                            file_entry[\"skeleton\"] = skeleton\n                        \n                        repo_data[\"files\"].append(file_entry)\n\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"\u274c Error: {rel_path} - {e}\")\n\n    print(f\"\ud83d\udcbe Compressing into the Void: {output_file}\")\n    with open(output_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        json.dump(repo_data, f, indent=2)\n    print(\"\u2705 Analysis Complete.\")\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    pack_repo(\".\", \"swarm_archive.json\")"

      "content": "defmodule SwarmBrain.MixProject do\n  use Mix.Project\n\n  def project do\n    [\n      app: :swarm_brain,\n      version: \"0.1.0\",\n      elixir: \"~> 1.15\",\n      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,\n      deps: deps(),\n      # \"repo_to_json.py\",\n      \"content\": \"import os\\nimport json\\nimport fnmatch\\nimport re\\nimport hashlib\\n\\n# --- CONFIGURATION ---\\nMAX_FILE_SIZE = 50 * 1024  # 50KB Limit\\nIGNORE_DIRS = X-RAY SCANNER (UPGRADED) ---\\ndef extract_elixir_skeleton(content):\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Extracts not just functions, but CONNECTIONS (aliases) and DATA SHAPES (structs).\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    skeleton = {\\n        \\\"modules\\\": [],\\n        \\\"dependencies\\\": [], # alias, import, require\\n        \\\"behaviors\\\": [],    # use, @behaviour\\n        \\\"structs\\\": [],      # defstruct keys\\n        \\\"public_functions\\\": []\\n    }\\n    \\n    # 1. Modules\\n    modules = re.findall(r'defmodule\\\\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\\\\._]*)', content)\\n    skeleton[\\\"modules\\\"] = modules\\n    \\n    # 2. Dependencies (The Tendons)\\n    # Finds \\\"alias SwarmBrain.Observation\\\" -> \\\"SwarmBrain.Observation\\\"\\n    aliases = re.findall(r'^\\\\s*alias\\\\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\\\\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)\\n    skeleton[\\\"dependencies\\\"].extend([f\\\"alias {a}\\\" for a in aliases])\\n    \\n    imports = re.findall(r'^\\\\s*import\\\\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\\\\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)\\n    skeleton[\\\"dependencies\\\"].extend([f\\\"import {i}\\\" for i in imports])\\n\\n    # 3. Behaviors (The Contracts)\\n    uses = re.findall(r'^\\\\s*use\\\\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\\\\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)\\n    skeleton[\\\"behaviors\\\"].extend([f\\\"use {u}\\\" for u in uses])\\n    \\n    behaviours = re.findall(r'^\\\\s*@behaviour\\\\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\\\\._]*)', content, re.MULTILINE)\\n    skeleton[\\\"behaviors\\\"].extend([f\\\"implements {b}\\\" for b in behaviours])\\n\\n    # 4. Data Shapes (The Bones)\\n    # This captures the raw string inside defstruct [...], cleaned up later by the LLM\\n    struct_match = re.search(r'defstruct\\\\s+\\\\[(.*?)\\\\]', content, re.DOTALL)\\n    if struct_match:\\n        # distinct keys roughly extracted\\n        raw_keys = struct_match.group(1)\\n        keys = re.findall(r':([a-z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)', raw_keys)\\n        skeleton[\\\"structs\\\"] = keys\\n\\n    # 5. Public Functions with Arity (The Muscles)\\n    # Matches \\\"def name(arg1, arg2) do\\\" or \\\"def name do\\\"\\n    # This is a heuristic, but good enough for architecture\\n    def_matches = re.finditer(r'^\\\\s*def\\\\s+([a-z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*[\\\\?!]?)\\\\s*(\\\\(.*\\\\))?\\\\s*(do|,)', content, re.MULTILINE)\\n    for m in def_matches:\\n        name = m.group(1)\\n        args_str = m.group(2)\\n        arity = 0\\n        if args_str:\\n            # count commas + 1, roughly\\n            arity = args_str.count(',') + 1\\n        skeleton[\\\"public_functions\\\"].append(f\\\"{name}/{arity}\\\")\\n\\n    return skeleton\\n\\ndef generate_tree(path, prefix=\\\"\\\"):\\n    tree_str = \\\"\\\"\\n    try:\\n        items = os.listdir(path)\\n    except PermissionError:\\n        return \\\"\\\"\\n    \\n    items.sort(key=lambda x: (not os.path.isdir(os.path.join(path, x)), x))\\n    \\n    filtered_items = []\\n    for item in items:\\n        if not any(fnmatch.fnmatch(item, pattern) for pattern in IGNORE_DIRS):\\n            filtered_items.append(item)\\n    \\n    pointers = [(\\\"\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 \\\" if i < len(filtered_items) - 1 else \\\"\\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 \\\") for i in range(len(filtered_items))]\\n\\n    for pointer, item in zip(pointers, filtered_items):\\n        full_path = os.path.join(path, item)\\n        tree_str += prefix + pointer + item + \\\"\\\\n\\\"\\n        \\n        if os.path.isdir(full_path):\\n            extension = \\\"\\u2502   \\\" if pointer == \\\"\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 \\\" else \\\"    \\\"\\n            tree_str += generate_tree(full_path, prefix + extension)\\n            \\n    return tree_str\\n\\ndef is_binary(file_path):\\n    try:\\n        with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:\\n            chunk = f.read(1024)\\n            if b'\\\\0' in chunk:\\n                return True\\n            try:\\n                chunk.decode('utf-8')\\n            except UnicodeDecodeError:\\n                return True\\n    except Exception:\\n        return True\\n    return False\\n\\ndef get_file_hash(file_path):\\n    h = hashlib.md5()\\n    try:\\n        with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:\\n            for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(4096), b\\\"\\\"):\\n                h.update(chunk)\\n        return h.hexdigest()[:8]\\n    except:\\n        return \\\"error\\\"\\n\\ndef pack_repo(repo_path, output_file):\\n    repo_data = {\\n        \\\"project_name\\\": os.path.basename(os.path.abspath(repo_path)),\\n        \\\"tree_view\\\": generate_tree(repo_path),\\n        \\\"structure\\\": [],\\n        \\\"files\\\": []\\n    }\\n\\n    print(f\\\"\\ud83d\\udc80 X-Raying Architecture: {repo_path}\\\")\\n\\n    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(repo_path):\\n        for i in range(len(dirs) - 1, -1, -1):\\n            d = dirs[i]\\n            if any(fnmatch.fnmatch(d, pattern) for pattern in IGNORE_DIRS):\\n                dirs.pop(i)\\n\\n        for file in files:\\n            file_path = os.path.join(root, file)\\n            rel_path = os.path.relpath(file_path, repo_path)\\n            \\n            _, ext = os.path.splitext(file)\\n            if ext in IGNORE_EXTENSIONS:\\n                continue\\n\\n            file_size = os.path.getsize(file_path)\\n            file_type = \\\"binary\\\" if is_binary(file_path) else \\\"text\\\"\\n            \\n            file_meta = {\\n                \\\"path\\\": rel_path,\\n                \\\"size\\\": file_size,\\n                \\\"type\\\": file_type,\\n                \\\"hash\\\": get_file_hash(file_path)\\n            }\\n            repo_data[\\\"structure\\\"].append(file_meta)\\n\\n            if file_type == \\\"text\\\":\\n                try:\\n                    with open(file_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore') as f:\\n                        if file_size > MAX_FILE_SIZE:\\n                            head_content = f.read(1000)\\n                            content = head_content + f\\\"\\\\n\\\\n[... \\u2702\\ufe0f VOID CUT: File too large ({file_size} bytes) ...]\\\"\\n                            print(f\\\"\\u26a0\\ufe0f Truncating: {rel_path}\\\")\\n                        else:\\n                            content = f.read()\\n                        \\n                        file_entry = {\\n                            \\\"path\\\": rel_path,\\n                            \\\"content\\\": content\\n                        }\\n\\n                        if ext in ['.ex', '.exs']:\\n                            file_entry[\\\"skeleton\\\"] = extract_elixir_skeleton(content)\\n                        \\n                        repo_data[\\\"files\\\"].append(file_entry)\\n\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"\\u274c Error: {rel_path} - {e}\\\")\\n\\n    print(f\\\"\\ud83d\\udcbe Compressing into the Void: {output_file}\\\")\\n    with open(output_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\\n        json.dump(repo_data, f, indent=2)\\n    print(\\\"\\u2705 Analysis Complete.\\\")\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    pack_repo(\\\".\\\", \\\"swarm_archive.json\\\")\"\n    },\n    {\n 0.229,\\n    0.224,\\n    0.225\\n  ],\\n  \\\"resample\\\": 3,\\n  \\\"size\\\": 224\\n}\\n\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"models/yolov8n/config.json\",\n      \"content\": \"{\\n  \\\"architectures\\\": [\\n    \\\"ResNetForImageClassification\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"depths\\\": [\\n    3,\\n    4,\\n    6,\\n    3\\n  ],\\n  \\\"downsample_in_first_stage\\\": false,\\n  \\\"embedding_size\\\": 64,\\n  \\\"hidden_act\\\": \\\"relu\\\",\\n  \\\"hidden_sizes\\\": [\\n    256,\\n    512,\\n    1024,\\n    2048\\n  ],\\n  \\\"id2label\\\": {\\n    \\\"0\\\": \\\"tench, Tinca tinca\\\",\\n    \\\"1\\\": \\\"goldfish, Carassius auratus\\\",\\n    \\\"2\\\": \\\"great white shark, white shark, man-eater, man-eating shark, Carcharodon carcharias\\\",\\n    \\\"3\\\": \\\"tiger shark, Galeocerdo cuvieri\\\",\\n    \\\"4\\\": \\\"hammerhead, hammerhead shark\\\",\\n    \\\"5\\\": \\\"electric ray, crampfish, numbfish, torpedo\\\",\\n    \\\"6\\\": \\\"stingray\\\",\\n    \\\"7\\\": \\\"cock\\\",\\n    \\\"8\\\": \\\"hen\\\",\\n    \\\"9\\\": \\\"ostrich, Struthio camelus\\\",\\n    \\\"10\\\": \\\"brambling, Fringilla montifringilla\\\",\\n    \\\"11\\\": \\\"goldfinch, Carduelis carduelis\\\",\\n    \\\"12\\\": \\\"house finch, linnet, Carpodacus mexicanus\\\",\\n    \\\"13\\\": \\\"junco, snowbird\\\",\\n    \\\"14\\\": \\\"indigo bunting, indigo finch, indigo bird, Passerina cyanea\\\",\\n    \\\"15\\\": \\\"robin, American robin, Turdus migratorius\\\",\\n    \\\"16\\\": \\\"bulbul\\\",\\n   \\n\\n[... \\u2702\\ufe0f VOID CUT: File too large (69556 bytes) ...]\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"lib/swarm_brain/protocol.ex\",\n      \"content\": \"defmodule SwarmBrain.Protocol do\\n  @moduledoc \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  The Compressor.\\n  Converts high-level thoughts (Structs) into low-level telegrams (Bytes).\\n  Protocol Format (14 Bytes Total):\\n  [HEADER(1)][ID(4)][LAT(4)][LON(4)][CLASS(1)]\\n  \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n  alias SwarmBrain.Observation\\n  @header 0xAA\\n\\n  # --- SERIALIZE (Struct -> Bytes) ---\\n  def serialize(%Observation{} = obs) do\\n    # 1. Map labels to simple integers (0=Unknown, 1=Human, 2=Tank)\\n    class_id = map_label_to_byte(List.first(obs.predictions))\\n\\n    # 2. Pack the bytes\\n    # We use float-32 (Little Endian) to save space.\\n    # Accuracy is ~1.5m, sufficient for swarm.\\n    <<\\n      @header,\\n      obs.id :: integer-32-little,\\n      obs.lat :: float-32-little,\\n      obs.lon :: float-32-little,\\n      class_id :: integer-8\\n    >>\\n  end\\n\\n  # --- DESERIALIZE (Bytes -> Struct) ---\\n  def deserialize(<<@header, id::integer-32-little, lat::float-32-little, lon::float-32-little, class_id::integer-8, _rest::binary>>) do\\n    label = map_byte_to_label(class_id)\\n\\n    %Observation{\\n      id: id,\\n      lat: lat,\\n      lon: lon,\\n      timestamp: DateTime.utc_now(),\\n      source_node: :remote_radio,\\n      predictions: [%{label: label, score: 1.0}] # We assume radio signals are confident\\n    }\\n  end\\n\\n  # Fallback for garbage noise\\n  def deserialize(_), do: :error\\n\\n  # --- MAPPING HELPERS ---\\n  defp map_label_to_byte(%{label: \\\"person\\\"}), do: 1\\n  defp map_label_to_byte(%{label: \\\"car\\\"}), do: 2\\n  defp map_label_to_byte(%{label: \\\"tank\\\"}), do: 3\\n  defp map_label_to_byte(_), do: 0 # Unknown\\n\\n  defp map_byte_to_label(1), do: \\\"person\\\"\\n  defp map_byte_to_label(2), do: \\\"car\\\"\\n  defp map_byte_to_label(3), do: \\\"tank\\\"\\n  defp map_byte_to_label(_), do: \\\"unknown\\\"\\nend\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [\n          \"SwarmBrain.Protocol\"\n        ],\n        \"dependencies\": [\n          \"alias SwarmBrain.Observation\"\n        ],\n        \"behaviors\": [],\n        \"structs\": [],\n        \"public_functions\": [\n          \"serialize/1\",\n          \"deserialize/6\",\n          \"deserialize/1\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"lib/swarm_brain/cortex.ex\",\n      \"content\": \"defmodule SwarmBrain.Cortex do\\n  alias SwarmBrain.Observation\\n\\n  # The Contract: \\\"Any brain plugged into this socket must implement analyze/1\\\"\\n  @callback analyze(%Observation{}) :: %Observation{}\\nend\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [\n          \"SwarmBrain.Cortex\"\n        ],\n        \"dependencies\": [\n          \"alias SwarmBrain.Observation\"\n        ],\n        \"behaviors\": [],\n        \"structs\": [],\n        \"public_functions\": []\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"lib/swarm_brain/antenna.ex\",\n      \"content\": \"defmodule SwarmBrain.Antenna do\\n  @moduledoc \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  The Receiver.\\n  It listens for incoming thoughts from other nodes and passes them to the Pipeline.\\n  \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  use GenServer\\n  require Logger\\n\\n  def start_link(_) do\\n    GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, %{}, name: __MODULE__)\\n  end\\n\\n  @impl true\\n  def init(state) do\\n    # Join the \\\"brains\\\" group so we can hear the signals\\n    :pg.join(:brains, self())\\n    Logger.info(\\\"\\ud83d\\udce1 Antenna is listening for remote signals...\\\")\\n    {:ok, state}\\n  end\\n\\n  # The Handle Info callback is the standard OTP way to receive messages\\n  @impl true\\n  def handle_info({:remote_process, observation}, state) do\\n    Logger.info(\\\"\\u26a1\\ufe0f Antenna picked up signal from #{observation.source_node}\\\")\\n\\n    # Pass it to the Unified Pipeline\\n    # We use Task.start so the Antenna is immediately ready for the next signal\\n    Task.start(fn ->\\n      SwarmBrain.Pipeline.ingest_remote_signal(observation)\\n    end)\\n\\n    {:noreply, state}\\n  end\\nend\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [\n          \"SwarmBrain.Antenna\"\n        ],\n        \"dependencies\": [],\n        \"behaviors\": [\n          \"use GenServer\"\n        ],\n        \"structs\": [],\n        \"public_functions\": [\n          \"start_link/1\",\n          \"init/1\",\n          \"handle_info/3\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"lib/swarm_brain/application.ex\",\n      \"content\": \"defmodule SwarmBrain.Application do\\n  use Application\\n  require Logger\\n\\n  @impl true\\n  def start(_type, _args) do\\n    # 1. Initialize Mnesia Schema/Table ON DISK\\n    SwarmBrain.Persistence.setup()\\n\\n    # 2. Join the \\\"Airwaves\\\" (WiFi Group)\\n    :pg.start_link()\\n\\n    children = [\\n      SwarmBrain.Discovery,\\n\\n      # \\ud83d\\udce1 NEW: The Wilderness Link (UART/LoRa)\\n      # We pass the serial port address (adjust \\\"/dev/ttyS0\\\" to your hardware)\\n      {SwarmBrain.Radio, [port: \\\"/dev/ttyS0\\\", speed: 420_000]},\\n\\n      # The Antenna listens for internal Erlang messages\\n      SwarmBrain.Antenna,\\n\\n      # The Vision Serving (The Neural Network Engine)\\n      {Nx.Serving, serving: vision_serving(), name: SwarmBrain.VisionServing},\\n\\n      SwarmBrain.Watchman\\n    ]\\n\\n    opts = [strategy: :one_for_one, name: SwarmBrain.Supervisor]\\n    Supervisor.start_link(children, opts)\\n  end\\n\\n  # --- VISION ENGINE SETUP ---\\n  defp vision_serving do\\n    local_path = Path.join([File.cwd!(), \\\"models\\\", \\\"yolov8n\\\"])\\n\\n    if File.exists?(local_path) do\\n      Logger.info(\\\"\\ud83e\\udde0 Loading Local Model from: #{local_path}\\\")\\n      {:ok, model} = Bumblebee.load_model({:local, local_path})\\n      {:ok, featurizer} = Bumblebee.load_featurizer({:local, local_path})\\n      Bumblebee.Vision.image_classification(model, featurizer)\\n    else\\n      Logger.warning(\\\"\\u26a0\\ufe0f Local model missing. Downloading ResNet-50...\\\")\\n      {:ok, model} = Bumblebee.load_model({:hf, \\\"microsoft/resnet-50\\\"})\\n      {:ok, featurizer} = Bumblebee.load_featurizer({:hf, \\\"microsoft/resnet-50\\\"})\\n      Bumblebee.Vision.image_classification(model, featurizer)\\n    end\\n  end\\nend\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [\n          \"SwarmBrain.Application\"\n        ],\n        \"dependencies\": [],\n        \"behaviors\": [\n          \"use Application\"\n        ],\n        \"structs\": [],\n        \"public_functions\": [\n          \"start/2\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"lib/swarm_brain/pipeline.ex\",\n      \"content\": \"defmodule SwarmBrain.Pipeline do\\n  @moduledoc \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  The Main Circuit Board.\\n  Connects Inputs -> Cortex -> Persistence.\\n  Now features \\\"Dual-Mode Switching\\\" (WiFi vs Radio).\\n  \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  require Logger\\n  alias SwarmBrain.{Eye, Location, Observation, Persistence, Radio}\\n\\n  @cortex Application.compile_env(:swarm_brain, :cortex_module)\\n\\n  # --- PUBLIC API ---\\n\\n  def run_local_sequence do\\n    Eye.capture()\\n    |> Location.stamp()\\n    |> process_signal()\\n  end\\n\\n  def ingest_remote_signal(observation) do\\n    process_signal(observation)\\n  end\\n\\n  def run_scout_sequence do\\n    Eye.capture()\\n    |> Location.stamp()\\n    |> broadcast_to_brain()\\n  end\\n\\n  # --- INTERNAL CIRCUIT ---\\n\\n  # 1. DOUBLE-THINK FIX: Skip analysis if predictions exist\\n  defp process_signal(%Observation{predictions: [_|_]} = obs) do\\n    persist_memory(obs)\\n  end\\n\\n  # 2. FRESH DATA: Analyze it\\n  defp process_signal(obs) do\\n    obs\\n    |> @cortex.analyze()\\n    |> persist_memory()\\n  end\\n\\n  def persist_memory(obs) do\\n    Persistence.save(obs)\\n    send(SwarmBrain.Watchman, {:observation_stored, obs})\\n    obs\\n  end\\n\\n  # --- THE DUAL-MODE SWITCH ---\\n  defp broadcast_to_brain(%Observation{} = obs) do\\n    # Check for High-Bandwidth Peers (WiFi)\\n    # Node.list() returns connected Erlang nodes.\\n    case Node.list() do\\n      [] ->\\n        # \\ud83c\\udf11 WILDERNESS MODE (No WiFi)\\n        # Send a compressed telegram via EMAX 2W\\n        Logger.info(\\\"\\ud83c\\udf11 No WiFi peers. Switching to RADIO TELEGRAM.\\\")\\n        Radio.broadcast(obs)\\n\\n        # We also process locally since we couldn't offload it\\n        process_signal(obs)\\n\\n      peers ->\\n        # \\u2600\\ufe0f CIVILIAN MODE (WiFi Present)\\n        # Send the full struct with image via TCP\\n        target = Enum.random(peers)\\n        Logger.info(\\\"\\u2600\\ufe0f WiFi peers found. Offloading task to #{inspect(target)}.\\\")\\n\\n        # Prune heavy image if needed (optional optimization)\\n        payload = Observation.prune_payload(obs)\\n        send({SwarmBrain.Antenna, target}, {:remote_process, payload})\\n    end\\n  end\\nend\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [\n          \"SwarmBrain.Pipeline\"\n        ],\n        \"dependencies\": [\n          \"alias SwarmBrain.\"\n        ],\n        \"behaviors\": [],\n        \"structs\": [],\n        \"public_functions\": [\n          \"run_local_sequence/0\",\n          \"ingest_remote_signal/1\",\n          \"run_scout_sequence/0\",\n          \"persist_memory/1\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"lib/swarm_brain/location.ex\",\n      \"content\": \"defmodule SwarmBrain.Location do\\n  alias SwarmBrain.Observation\\n\\n  def stamp(%Observation{} = obs) do\\n    # In the future: call mavlink or gpsd here\\n    {lat, lon, alt} = get_current_gps()\\n\\n    %{obs | lat: lat, lon: lon, alt: alt}\\n  end\\n\\n  defp get_current_gps do\\n    # Placeholder: Lviv High Castle\\n    {49.8481, 24.0397, 350.0}\\n  end\\nend\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [\n          \"SwarmBrain.Location\"\n        ],\n        \"dependencies\": [\n          \"alias SwarmBrain.Observation\"\n        ],\n        \"behaviors\": [],\n        \"structs\": [],\n        \"public_functions\": [\n          \"stamp/1\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"lib/swarm_brain/radio.ex\",\n      \"content\": \"defmodule SwarmBrain.Radio do\\n  @moduledoc \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  The Wilderness Link.\\n  Talks directly to the EMAX 2W module via UART.\\n  No TCP. No Handshakes. Just raw metal.\\n  \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  use GenServer\\n  require Logger\\n  alias Circuits.UART\\n  alias SwarmBrain.{Protocol, Pipeline}\\n\\n  # --- API ---\\n\\n  def start_link(opts) do\\n    GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, opts, name: __MODULE__)\\n  end\\n\\n  def broadcast(observation) do\\n    GenServer.cast(__MODULE__, {:tx, observation})\\n  end\\n\\n  # --- CALLBACKS ---\\n\\n  @impl true\\n  def init(opts) do\\n    port = Keyword.get(opts, :port, \\\"/dev/ttyS0\\\")\\n    speed = Keyword.get(opts, :speed, 420_000)\\n\\n    {:ok, pid} = UART.start_link()\\n\\n    # Configure for Raw Binary Mode\\n    # active: true means messages are sent to this process as {:circuits_uart, ...}\\n\\n    # SOFT FAIL FIX: We try to open. If it fails, we stay alive as a dummy.\\n    case UART.open(pid, port, speed: speed, active: true) do\\n      :ok ->\\n        Logger.info(\\\"\\ud83d\\udce1 RADIO ONLINE: Connected to #{port} at #{speed} baud.\\\")\\n        {:ok, %{uart: pid, port: port, active: true}}\\n\\n      {:error, reason} ->\\n        Logger.warning(\\\"\\u26a0\\ufe0f RADIO OFFLINE: Could not open #{port} (#{inspect(reason)}). Entering Simulation Mode.\\\")\\n        # We return :ok anyway, so the Supervisor doesn't crash the app\\n        {:ok, %{uart: nil, port: port, active: false}}\\n    end\\n  end\\n\\n  # RECEIVING (RX): Incoming Bytes from EMAX\\n  @impl true\\n  def handle_info({:circuits_uart, _port, binary_data}, state) do\\n    case Protocol.deserialize(binary_data) do\\n      %SwarmBrain.Observation{} = obs ->\\n        Logger.info(\\\"\\u26a1\\ufe0f RADIO RX: Received telegram ID:#{obs.id}\\\")\\n        Pipeline.ingest_remote_signal(obs)\\n      :error ->\\n        :ok\\n    end\\n    {:noreply, state}\\n  end\\n\\n  # SENDING (TX): Outgoing Observation\\n  @impl true\\n  def handle_cast({:tx, obs}, %{active: true} = state) do\\n    packet = Protocol.serialize(obs)\\n    UART.write(state.uart, packet)\\n    {:noreply, state}\\n  end\\n\\n  # SENDING (TX): Dummy Mode (Radio hardware missing)\\n  def handle_cast({:tx, obs}, %{active: false} = state) do\\n    Logger.debug(\\\"\\ud83d\\udc7b [SIMULATION] Radio TX would send: #{inspect(obs.id)}\\\")\\n    # Here you could potentially write to a file to simulate transmission\\n    {:noreply, state}\\n  end\\nend\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [\n          \"SwarmBrain.Radio\"\n        ],\n        \"dependencies\": [\n          \"alias Circuits.UART\",\n          \"alias SwarmBrain.\"\n        ],\n        \"behaviors\": [\n          \"use GenServer\"\n        ],\n        \"structs\": [],\n        \"public_functions\": [\n          \"start_link/1\",\n          \"broadcast/1\",\n          \"init/1\",\n          \"handle_info/4\",\n          \"handle_cast/3\",\n          \"handle_cast/3\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"lib/swarm_brain/eye.ex\",\n      \"content\": \"defmodule SwarmBrain.Eye do\\n  @moduledoc \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  The Input Stage.\\n  Responsible for hardware interaction and signal conditioning.\\n  \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  require Logger\\n  alias SwarmBrain.Observation\\n\\n  # 1. THE REVOLUTIONARY PIPELINE STEP\\n  # This function fits perfectly into a |> pipe.\\n  def capture(_opts \\\\\\\\ []) do\\n    # Emit a 'signal start' pulse for the dashboard\\n    :telemetry.execute([:swarm, :eye, :capture], %{count: 1})\\n\\n    image_data = get_hardware_image()\\n\\n    # Return the pure signal\\n    Observation.new(image_data)\\n  end\\n\\n  # --- HARDWARE ABSTRACTION ---\\n\\n  defp get_hardware_image do\\n    # In a real scenario, this detects if we are Linux/Mac/RPi\\n    # For now, we use the robust \\\"Task\\\" approach to prevent hanging.\\n    task = Task.async(fn ->\\n      # Simulating hardware latency\\n      Process.sleep(100)\\n      perform_shutter()\\n    end)\\n\\n    case Task.yield(task, 2000) do\\n      {:ok, binary} -> binary\\n      nil ->\\n        Task.shutdown(task, :brutal_kill)\\n        Logger.error(\\\"\\u26a0\\ufe0f Camera Hardware Timeout! Returning static.\\\")\\n        read_test_pattern()\\n    end\\n  end\\n\\n  defp perform_shutter do\\n    cond do\\n      File.exists?(\\\"eye.jpg\\\") -> File.read!(\\\"eye.jpg\\\")\\n      true ->\\n        # Fallback to a 1x1 pixel black dot if nothing exists\\n        <<0::size(8)>>\\n    end\\n  end\\n\\n  defp read_test_pattern, do: \\\"PLACEHOLDER_BINARY\\\"\\nend\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [\n          \"SwarmBrain.Eye\"\n        ],\n        \"dependencies\": [\n          \"alias SwarmBrain.Observation\"\n        ],\n        \"behaviors\": [],\n        \"structs\": [],\n        \"public_functions\": [\n          \"capture/1\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"lib/swarm_brain/watchman.ex\",\n      \"content\": \"defmodule SwarmBrain.Watchman do\\n  use GenServer\\n  require Logger\\n\\n  @log_file \\\"brain_observations.csv\\\"\\n\\n  # --- Client API ---\\n\\n  def start_link(_) do\\n    GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, %{}, name: __MODULE__)\\n  end\\n\\n  # --- Server Callbacks ---\\n\\n  @impl true\\n  def init(_) do\\n    # 1. Cluster Monitoring\\n    :net_kernel.monitor_nodes(true)\\n\\n    # 2. Subscribe to Mnesia (Legacy/Redundant backup)\\n    :mnesia.subscribe({:table, :sightings, :detailed})\\n\\n    # 3. Setup CSV\\n    if !File.exists?(@log_file), do: File.write!(@log_file, \\\"timestamp,event,details\\\\n\\\")\\n\\n    # REMOVED: schedule_vision_check() - We are now Passive listeners!\\n\\n    Logger.info(\\\"\\ud83d\\udd75\\ufe0f Watchman is active. Waiting for Pipeline signals.\\\")\\n    {:ok, %{nodes: Node.list()}}\\n  end\\n\\n  # --- Handling the Swarm Events (Node Up/Down) ---\\n\\n  @impl true\\n  def handle_info({:nodeup, node}, state) do\\n    log_event(\\\"CONNECTION\\\", \\\"Swarm became one with node: #{node}\\\")\\n    IO.puts(\\\"\\u2728 [SWARM] Node connected: #{node}. Symmetrical state syncing...\\\")\\n    {:noreply, %{state | nodes: [node | state.nodes]}}\\n  end\\n\\n  @impl true\\n  def handle_info({:nodedown, node}, state) do\\n    log_event(\\\"DISCONNECT\\\", \\\"Lost connection to node: #{node}\\\")\\n    IO.puts(\\\"\\ud83d\\udea8 [SWARM] Node lost: #{node}. Entering autonomous mode.\\\")\\n    {:noreply, %{state | nodes: List.delete(state.nodes, node)}}\\n  end\\n\\n  # --- NEW: Handling the Pipeline Signal ---\\n  # This receives the struct from SwarmBrain.Pipeline\\n  @impl true\\n  def handle_info({:observation_stored, obs}, state) do\\n    # 1. Unpack the signal\\n    %{predictions: preds, lat: lat, lon: lon, source_node: node} = obs\\n\\n    # 2. Format the output\\n    top_label = case preds do\\n      [top | _] -> \\\"#{top.label} (#{Float.round(top.score * 100, 1)}%)\\\"\\n      [] -> \\\"Unknown Object\\\"\\n    end\\n\\n    details = \\\"Detected #{top_label} at [#{lat}, #{lon}] by #{node}\\\"\\n\\n    # 3. Log to CSV and Screen\\n    log_event(\\\"SIGHTING\\\", details)\\n    IO.puts(\\\"\\ud83d\\udd14 [WATCHMAN] #{details}\\\")\\n\\n    {:noreply, state}\\n  end\\n\\n  # Fallback for other Mnesia events\\n  @impl true\\n  def handle_info({:mnesia_table_event, _}, state), do: {:noreply, state}\\n\\n  # Catch-all\\n  @impl true\\n  def handle_info(_msg, state), do: {:noreply, state}\\n\\n  # --- Private Helpers ---\\n\\n  defp log_event(event_type, details) do\\n    timestamp = DateTime.utc_now() |> DateTime.to_iso8601()\\n    clean_details = String.replace(details, \\\"\\\\\\\"\\\", \\\"'\\\")\\n    entry = \\\"#{timestamp},#{event_type},\\\\\\\"#{clean_details}\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\"\\n    File.write!(@log_file, entry, [:append])\\n  end\\nend\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [\n          \"SwarmBrain.Watchman\"\n        ],\n        \"dependencies\": [],\n        \"behaviors\": [\n          \"use GenServer\"\n        ],\n        \"structs\": [],\n        \"public_functions\": [\n          \"start_link/1\",\n          \"init/1\",\n          \"handle_info/3\",\n          \"handle_info/3\",\n          \"handle_info/3\",\n          \"handle_info/3\",\n          \"handle_info/2\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"lib/swarm_brain/discovery.ex\",\n      \"content\": \"defmodule SwarmBrain.Discovery do\\n  @moduledoc \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  The Civilian Link.\\n  Manages WiFi/Erlang Distribution connections.\\n  If this fails, the Pipeline automatically falls back to Radio.\\n  \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  use GenServer\\n  require Logger\\n  alias SwarmBrain.Persistence\\n\\n  @interval 5_000\\n\\n  def start_link(opts) do\\n    GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, opts, name: __MODULE__)\\n  end\\n\\n  @impl true\\n  def init(_opts) do\\n    send(self(), :connect)\\n    {:ok, %{nodes: list_target_nodes()}}\\n  end\\n\\n  @impl true\\n  def handle_info(:connect, state) do\\n    Enum.each(state.nodes, fn node_name ->\\n      if node_name != Node.self() do\\n        Node.connect(node_name)\\n      end\\n    end)\\n\\n    # Check who is actually alive for logging\\n    connected = Node.list()\\n    if connected != [] do\\n      Logger.debug(\\\"\\ud83c\\udf10 WiFi Mesh Active: #{inspect(connected)}\\\")\\n      # Sync databases if we have a connection\\n      Enum.each(connected, &Persistence.add_node_to_cluster/1)\\n    end\\n\\n    Process.send_after(self(), :connect, @interval)\\n    {:noreply, state}\\n  end\\n\\n  defp list_target_nodes do\\n    # Symmetrical List of known IP addresses\\n    [:\\\"brain@192.168.1.147\\\", :\\\"brain@192.168.1.243\\\"]\\n  end\\nend\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [\n          \"SwarmBrain.Discovery\"\n        ],\n        \"dependencies\": [\n          \"alias SwarmBrain.Persistence\"\n        ],\n        \"behaviors\": [\n          \"use GenServer\"\n        ],\n        \"structs\": [],\n        \"public_functions\": [\n          \"start_link/1\",\n          \"init/1\",\n          \"handle_info/2\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"lib/swarm_brain/observation.ex\",\n      \"content\": \"defmodule SwarmBrain.Observation do\\n  @moduledoc \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  The Standard Protocol.\\n  This struct is the \\\"Signal\\\" that flows through the Class A Amplifier.\\n  Includes logic for \\\"Heavy Packet\\\" optimization (Payload Pruning).\\n  \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  defstruct [\\n    :id,              # Unique UUID for the memory\\n    :image_binary,    # The raw visual data (Heavy)\\n    :timestamp,       # When it was seen (UTC)\\n    :lat,             # GPS Latitude\\n    :lon,             # GPS Longitude\\n    :alt,             # GPS Altitude\\n    :predictions,     # The YOLO output (List of maps)\\n    :source_node      # Which device took the picture\\n  ]\\n\\n  def new(image_binary, node_name \\\\\\\\ Node.self()) do\\n    %__MODULE__{\\n      id: :erlang.unique_integer([:positive, :monotonic]),\\n      image_binary: image_binary,\\n      timestamp: DateTime.utc_now(),\\n      source_node: node_name,\\n      predictions: []\\n    }\\n  end\\n\\n  @doc \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  The Heavy Packet Optimization.\\n  Removes the heavy JPEG binary from the struct before network transmission.\\n  The receiving node will have the coordinates and label, but not the picture.\\n  If they need the picture, they must request it via ID (Lazy Loading - Future Feature).\\n  \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  def prune_payload(%__MODULE__{} = obs) do\\n    %{obs | image_binary: nil}\\n  end\\nend\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [\n          \"SwarmBrain.Observation\"\n        ],\n        \"dependencies\": [],\n        \"behaviors\": [],\n        \"structs\": [\n          \"id\",\n          \"image_binary\",\n          \"timestamp\",\n          \"lat\",\n          \"lon\",\n          \"alt\",\n          \"predictions\",\n          \"source_node\"\n        ],\n        \"public_functions\": [\n          \"new/2\",\n          \"prune_payload/1\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"lib/swarm_brain/persistence.ex\",\n      \"content\": \"defmodule SwarmBrain.Persistence do\\n  @moduledoc \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  The Long-Term Memory.\\n  Implements \\\"Genesis Boot\\\" to handle Split-Brain scenarios automatically.\\n  Includes \\\"Ghost in the Shell\\\" Black Box writing for crash resilience.\\n  \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  require Logger\\n  alias SwarmBrain.Observation\\n\\n  @table :sightings\\n  # Hardcoded peer list for discovery (Symmetrical - both have same list)\\n  @peers [:\\\"brain@192.168.1.147\\\", :\\\"brain@192.168.1.243\\\"]\\n  @blackbox_log \\\"blackbox.log\\\"\\n\\n  # --- SETUP (The Genesis Protocol) ---\\n\\n  def setup do\\n    # 1. Stop Mnesia to ensure clean state\\n    :mnesia.stop()\\n\\n    # 2. Check for Life in the Universe\\n    # We filter out ourselves and ping the others.\\n    others = @peers -- [node()]\\n    living_peers = Enum.filter(others, fn n -> Node.ping(n) == :pong end)\\n\\n    if Enum.empty?(living_peers) do\\n      # \\ud83d\\udc51 CASE A: Solitary Node (Leader)\\n      # No one else is here. I trust my own disk.\\n      Logger.info(\\\"\\ud83d\\udc51 No active swarm detected. Loading local memory from disk.\\\")\\n      :mnesia.create_schema([node()])\\n      :mnesia.start()\\n    else\\n      # \\ud83e\\udd1d CASE B: Joining Existing Swarm (Follower)\\n      # The swarm is alive. My local schema might be stale/conflicting.\\n      # \\\"Zen Mode\\\": I empty my cup so it can be filled.\\n      Logger.info(\\\"\\ud83d\\udc1d Active swarm detected #{inspect(living_peers)}. Wiping local schema to join.\\\")\\n\\n      # WIPE SELF (This replaces the risky recursive delete)\\n      :mnesia.delete_schema([node()])\\n      :mnesia.start()\\n\\n      # Connect to the swarm database\\n      :mnesia.change_config(:extra_db_nodes, living_peers)\\n\\n      # Ensure we have the table definition\\n      case :mnesia.wait_for_tables([@table], 5000) do\\n         :ok -> :ok\\n         _ ->\\n            # If table doesn't exist yet in swarm, we might need to copy it\\n            :ok\\n      end\\n    end\\n\\n    init_table()\\n  end\\n\\n  defp init_table do\\n    # Create the table if it doesn't exist (On Disk)\\n    case :mnesia.create_table(@table, [attributes: [:uuid, :data], disc_copies: [node()]]) do\\n      {:atomic, :ok} -> Logger.info(\\\"\\u2705 Memory table created.\\\")\\n      {:aborted, {:already_exists, _}} -> Logger.info(\\\"\\u2705 Memory table loaded.\\\")\\n      other -> Logger.error(\\\"\\u274c Mnesia Table Init: #{inspect(other)}\\\")\\n    end\\n\\n    :mnesia.wait_for_tables([@table], 5000)\\n  end\\n\\n  # --- CLUSTERING (The Healer) ---\\n\\n  def add_node_to_cluster(target_node) do\\n    # This now handles the \\\"Idempotent\\\" log cleanup you asked for\\n    :mnesia.change_config(:extra_db_nodes, [target_node])\\n\\n    # A. Push (Ask them to copy)\\n    case :rpc.call(target_node, :mnesia, :add_table_copy, [@table, target_node, :disc_copies]) do\\n      {:atomic, :ok} -> Logger.info(\\\"\\u2705 Remote: Memory replicated to #{target_node}\\\")\\n      {:aborted, {:already_exists, _}} -> :ok # Silent success\\n      other -> Logger.warning(\\\"\\u26a0\\ufe0f Remote Sync: #{inspect(other)}\\\")\\n    end\\n\\n    # B. Pull (Ensure I have copy)\\n    case :mnesia.add_table_copy(@table, Node.self(), :disc_copies) do\\n      {:atomic, :ok} -> Logger.info(\\\"\\u2705 Local: Memory replicated from Swarm\\\")\\n      {:aborted, {:already_exists, _}} -> :ok # Silent success\\n      other -> Logger.warning(\\\"\\u26a0\\ufe0f Local Sync: #{inspect(other)}\\\")\\n    end\\n  end\\n\\n  # --- SAVE & RETRIEVE (With Black Box) ---\\n\\n  def save(%Observation{} = obs) do\\n    {label, score} = case obs.predictions do\\n      [top | _] -> {top.label, top.score}\\n      [] -> {\\\"unknown\\\", 0.0}\\n    end\\n\\n    # 1. GHOST IN THE SHELL: Secure Write\\n    # Write to raw log immediately to survive power loss\\n    secure_log_entry = \\\"#{DateTime.to_string(obs.timestamp)},#{label},#{score},#{obs.lat},#{obs.lon},#{obs.source_node}\\\\n\\\"\\n    secure_write(secure_log_entry)\\n\\n    # 2. Polite Write (Mnesia)\\n    trans = fn ->\\n      :mnesia.write({@table, obs.id, obs})\\n    end\\n\\n    case :mnesia.transaction(trans) do\\n      {:atomic, :ok} -> :ok\\n      other -> Logger.error(\\\"Failed to persist memory: #{inspect(other)}\\\")\\n    end\\n  end\\n\\n  def get_recent_sightings(limit \\\\\\\\ 10) do\\n    # A simple dirty read for the dashboard\\n    # In production, use :mnesia.select for better querying\\n    keys = :mnesia.dirty_all_keys(@table)\\n\\n    keys\\n    |> Enum.take(-limit) # Take last N\\n    |> Enum.map(fn key ->\\n      [{_, _, obs}] = :mnesia.dirty_read(@table, key)\\n      obs\\n    end)\\n  end\\n\\n  # --- PRIVATE: THE BLACK BOX WRITER ---\\n\\n  defp secure_write(data) do\\n    # Force the OS to write to physical silicon IMMEDIATELY.\\n    # It slows down the system, but ensures the memory survives the crash.\\n    # O_SYNC / :sync flag bypasses the OS buffer cache.\\n    case File.write(@blackbox_log, data, [:append, :sync]) do\\n      :ok -> :ok\\n      {:error, reason} -> Logger.error(\\\"\\ud83d\\udd25 BLACKBOX FAILURE: #{inspect(reason)}\\\")\\n    end\\n  end\\nend\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [\n          \"SwarmBrain.Persistence\"\n        ],\n        \"dependencies\": [\n          \"alias SwarmBrain.Observation\"\n        ],\n        \"behaviors\": [],\n        \"structs\": [],\n        \"public_functions\": [\n          \"setup/0\",\n          \"add_node_to_cluster/1\",\n          \"save/1\",\n          \"get_recent_sightings/1\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"lib/swarm_brain/cortex/resnet.ex\",\n      \"content\": \"defmodule SwarmBrain.Cortex.ResNet do\\n  # 1. Adopt the Behaviour (The Law)\\n  @behaviour SwarmBrain.Cortex\\n\\n  alias SwarmBrain.Observation\\n  require Logger\\n\\n  # 2. THIS IS YOUR ORIGINAL CODE (SAFE AND SOUND)\\n  def analyze(%Observation{image_binary: bin} = obs) do\\n    :telemetry.execute([:swarm, :cortex, :think], %{count: 1})\\n    Logger.info(\\\"\\ud83e\\udde0 Cortex (ResNet) is thinking...\\\")\\n\\n    # ... (All your original logic for StbImage and Nx.Serving stays here) ...\\n    tensor =\\n      bin\\n      |> StbImage.read_binary!()\\n      |> StbImage.to_nx()\\n\\n    %{predictions: preds} = Nx.Serving.batched_run(SwarmBrain.VisionServing, tensor)\\n\\n    %{obs | predictions: preds}\\n  end\\nend\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [\n          \"SwarmBrain.Cortex.ResNet\"\n        ],\n        \"dependencies\": [\n          \"alias SwarmBrain.Observation\"\n        ],\n        \"behaviors\": [\n          \"implements SwarmBrain.Cortex\"\n        ],\n        \"structs\": [],\n        \"public_functions\": [\n          \"analyze/1\"\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"config/config.exs\",\n      \"content\": \"import Config\\n\\n# We plug the ResNet module into the Cortex socket\\nconfig :swarm_brain, :cortex_module, SwarmBrain.Cortex.ResNet\\n\\n# 1. Configure the Target\\n# If you are on the INTEL laptop without an NVIDIA card:\\nconfig :exla, :default_client, :host\\n\\n# \\u26a0\\ufe0f IF you manage to get an eGPU or NVIDIA card working later:\\n# config :exla, :default_client, :cuda\\n# config :exla, :clients,\\n#   cuda: [platform: :cuda]\\n\\n# 3. CRDT Sync Interval\\nconfig :delta_crdt, :sync_interval, 50 # Sync fast (50ms) for flight data\\n\",\n      \"skeleton\": {\n        \"modules\": [],\n        \"dependencies\": [\n          \"import Config\"\n        ],\n        \"behaviors\": [],\n        \"structs\": [],\n        \"public_functions\": []\n      }\n    }\n  ]\n}"

      "path": "lib/swarm_brain/protocol.ex",
      "content": "defmodule SwarmBrain.Protocol do\n  @moduledoc \"\"\"\n  The Compressor.\n  Converts high-level thoughts (Structs) into low-level telegrams (Bytes).\n  Protocol Format (14 Bytes Total):\n  [HEADER(1)][ID(4)][LAT(4)][LON(4)][CLASS(1)]\n  \"\"\"\n\n  alias SwarmBrain.Observation\n  @header 0xAA\n\n  # --- SERIALIZE (Struct -> Bytes) ---\n  def serialize(%Observation{} = obs) do\n    # 1. Map labels to simple integers (0=Unknown, 1=Human, 2=Tank)\n    class_id = map_label_to_byte(List.first(obs.predictions))\n\n    # 2. Pack the bytes\n    # We use float-32 (Little Endian) to save space.\n    # Accuracy is ~1.5m, sufficient for swarm.\n    <<\n      @header,\n      obs.id :: integer-32-little,\n      obs.lat :: float-32-little,\n      obs.lon :: float-32-little,\n      class_id :: integer-8\n    >>\n  end\n\n  # --- DESERIALIZE (Bytes -> Struct) ---\n  def deserialize(<<@header, id::integer-32-little, lat::float-32-little, lon::float-32-little, class_id::integer-8, _rest::binary>>) do\n    label = map_byte_to_label(class_id)\n\n    %Observation{\n      id: id,\n      lat: lat,\n      lon: lon,\n      timestamp: DateTime.utc_now(),\n      source_node: :remote_radio,\n      predictions: [%{label: label, score: 1.0}] # We assume radio signals are confident\n    }\n  end\n\n  # Fallback for garbage noise\n  def deserialize(_), do: :error\n\n  # --- MAPPING HELPERS ---\n  defp map_label_to_byte(%{label: \"person\"}), do: 1\n  defp map_label_to_byte(%{label: \"car\"}), do: 2\n  defp map_label_to_byte(%{label: \"tank\"}), do: 3\n  defp map_label_to_byte(_), do: 0 # Unknown\n\n  defp map_byte_to_label(1), do: \"person\"\n  defp map_byte_to_label(2), do: \"car\"\n  defp map_byte_to_label(3), do: \"tank\"\n  defp map_byte_to_label(_), do: \"unknown\"\nend\n",
      "skeleton": {
        "modules": [
          "SwarmBrain.Protocol"
        ],
        "dependencies": [
          "alias SwarmBrain.Observation"
        ],

        "structs": [],

          "serialize/1",
          "deserialize/6",
          "deserialize/1"
        ],
        "todos": []
      }
    },

      "path": "lib/swarm_brain/antenna.ex",
      "content": "defmodule SwarmBrain.Antenna do\n  @moduledoc \"\"\"\n  The Receiver.\n  It listens for incoming thoughts from other nodes and passes them to the Pipeline.\n  \"\"\"\n  use GenServer\n  require Logger\n\n  def start_link(_) do\n    GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, %{}, name: __MODULE__)\n  end\n\n  @impl true\n  def init(state) do\n    # Join the \"brains\" group so we can hear the signals\n    :pg.join(:brains, self())\n    Logger.info(\"\ud83d\udce1 Antenna is listening for remote signals...\")\n    {:ok, state}\n  end\n\n  # The Handle Info callback is the standard OTP way to receive messages\n  @impl true\n  def handle_info({:remote_process, observation}, state) do\n    Logger.info(\"\u26a1\ufe0f Antenna picked up signal from #{observation.source_node}\")\n\n    # Pass it to the Unified Pipeline\n    # We use Task.start so the Antenna is immediately ready for the next signal\n    Task.start(fn ->\n      SwarmBrain.Pipeline.ingest_remote_signal(observation)\n    end)\n\n    {:noreply, state}\n  end\nend\n",
      "skeleton": {
        "modules": [
          "SwarmBrain.Antenna"
        ],
        "dependencies": [],
        "behaviors": [
          "use GenServer"
        ],
        "structs": [],

          "init/1",

        ],
        "todos": []
      }

      "path": "lib/swarm_brain/application.ex",
      "content": "defmodule SwarmBrain.Application do\n  use Application\n  require Logger\n\n  @impl true\n  def start(_type, _args) do\n    # 1. Initialize Mnesia Schema/Table ON DISK\n    SwarmBrain.Persistence.setup()\n\n    # 2. Join the \"Airwaves\" (WiFi Group)\n    :pg.start_link()\n\n    children = [\n      SwarmBrain.Discovery,\n\n      # \ud83d\udce1 NEW: The Wilderness Link (UART/LoRa)\n      # We pass the serial port address (adjust \"/dev/ttyS0\" to your hardware)\n      {SwarmBrain.Radio, [port: \"/dev/ttyS0\", speed: 420_000]},\n\n      # The Antenna listens for internal Erlang messages\n      SwarmBrain.Antenna,\n\n      # The Vision Serving (The Neural Network Engine)\n      {Nx.Serving, serving: vision_serving(), name: SwarmBrain.VisionServing},\n\n      SwarmBrain.Watchman\n    ]\n\n    opts = [strategy: :one_for_one, name: SwarmBrain.Supervisor]\n    Supervisor.start_link(children, opts)\n  end\n\n  # --- VISION ENGINE SETUP ---\n  defp vision_serving do\n    local_path = Path.join([File.cwd!(), \"models\", \"yolov8n\"])\n\n    if File.exists?(local_path) do\n      Logger.info(\"\ud83e\udde0 Loading Local Model from: #{local_path}\")\n      {:ok, model} = Bumblebee.load_model({:local, local_path})\n      {:ok, featurizer} = Bumblebee.load_featurizer({:local, local_path})\n      Bumblebee.Vision.image_classification(model, featurizer)\n    else\n      Logger.warning(\"\u26a0\ufe0f Local model missing. Downloading ResNet-50...\")\n      {:ok, model} = Bumblebee.load_model({:hf, \"microsoft/resnet-50\"})\n      {:ok, featurizer} = Bumblebee.load_featurizer({:hf, \"microsoft/resnet-50\"})\n      Bumblebee.Vision.image_classification(model, featurizer)\n    end\n  end\nend\n",

          "use Application"
        ],

          "start/2"
        ],
        "todos": []
      }

      "path": "lib/swarm_brain/pipeline.ex",
      "content": "defmodule SwarmBrain.Pipeline do\n  @moduledoc \"\"\"\n  The Main Circuit Board.\n  Connects Inputs -> Cortex -> Persistence.\n  Now features \"Dual-Mode Switching\" (WiFi vs Radio).\n  \"\"\"\n  require Logger\n  alias SwarmBrain.{Eye, Location, Observation, Persistence, Radio}\n\n  @cortex Application.compile_env(:swarm_brain, :cortex_module)\n\n  # --- PUBLIC API ---\n\n  def run_local_sequence do\n    Eye.capture()\n    |> Location.stamp()\n    |> process_signal()\n  end\n\n  def ingest_remote_signal(observation) do\n    process_signal(observation)\n  end\n\n  def run_scout_sequence do\n    Eye.capture()\n    |> Location.stamp()\n    |> broadcast_to_brain()\n  end\n\n  # --- INTERNAL CIRCUIT ---\n\n  # 1. DOUBLE-THINK FIX: Skip analysis if predictions exist\n  defp process_signal(%Observation{predictions: [_|_]} = obs) do\n    persist_memory(obs)\n  end\n\n  # 2. FRESH DATA: Analyze it\n  defp process_signal(obs) do\n    obs\n    |> @cortex.analyze()\n    |> persist_memory()\n  end\n\n  def persist_memory(obs) do\n    Persistence.save(obs)\n    send(SwarmBrain.Watchman, {:observation_stored, obs})\n    obs\n  end\n\n  # --- THE DUAL-MODE SWITCH ---\n  defp broadcast_to_brain(%Observation{} = obs) do\n    # Check for High-Bandwidth Peers (WiFi)\n    # Node.list() returns connected Erlang nodes.\n    case Node.list() do\n      [] ->\n        # \ud83c\udf11 WILDERNESS MODE (No WiFi)\n        # Send a compressed telegram via EMAX 2W\n        Logger.info(\"\ud83c\udf11 No WiFi peers. Switching to RADIO TELEGRAM.\")\n        Radio.broadcast(obs)\n\n        # We also process locally since we couldn't offload it\n        process_signal(obs)\n\n      peers ->\n        # \u2600\ufe0f CIVILIAN MODE (WiFi Present)\n        # Send the full struct with image via TCP\n        target = Enum.random(peers)\n        Logger.info(\"\u2600\ufe0f WiFi peers found. Offloading task to #{inspect(target)}.\")\n\n        # Prune heavy image if needed (optional optimization)\n        payload = Observation.prune_payload(obs)\n        send({SwarmBrain.Antenna, target}, {:remote_process, payload})\n    end\n  end\nend\n",
      "skeleton": {
        "modules": [
          "SwarmBrain.Pipeline"
        ],
        "dependencies": [
          "alias SwarmBrain."
        ],
        "behaviors": [],
        "structs": [],
        "public_functions": [
          "run_local_sequence/0",
          "ingest_remote_signal/1",
          "run_scout_sequence/0",
          "persist_memory/1"
        ],
        "todos": []
      }
    },

      "path": "lib/swarm_brain/location.ex",
      "content": "defmodule SwarmBrain.Location do\n  alias SwarmBrain.Observation\n\n  def stamp(%Observation{} = obs) do\n    # In the future: call mavlink or gpsd here\n    {lat, lon, alt} = get_current_gps()\n\n    %{obs | lat: lat, lon: lon, alt: alt}\n  end\n\n  defp get_current_gps do\n    # Placeholder: Lviv High Castle\n    {49.8481, 24.0397, 350.0}\n  end\nend\n",

      "path": "lib/swarm_brain/radio.ex",
      "content": "defmodule SwarmBrain.Radio do\n  @moduledoc \"\"\"\n  The Wilderness Link.\n  Talks directly to the EMAX 2W module via UART.\n  No TCP. No Handshakes. Just raw metal.\n  \"\"\"\n  use GenServer\n  require Logger\n  alias Circuits.UART\n  alias SwarmBrain.{Protocol, Pipeline}\n\n  # --- API ---\n\n  def start_link(opts) do\n    GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, opts, name: __MODULE__)\n  end\n\n  def broadcast(observation) do\n    GenServer.cast(__MODULE__, {:tx, observation})\n  end\n\n  # --- CALLBACKS ---\n\n  @impl true\n  def init(opts) do\n    port = Keyword.get(opts, :port, \"/dev/ttyS0\")\n    speed = Keyword.get(opts, :speed, 420_000)\n\n    {:ok, pid} = UART.start_link()\n\n    # Configure for Raw Binary Mode\n    # active: true means messages are sent to this process as {:circuits_uart, ...}\n\n    # SOFT FAIL FIX: We try to open. If it fails, we stay alive as a dummy.\n    case UART.open(pid, port, speed: speed, active: true) do\n      :ok ->\n        Logger.info(\"\ud83d\udce1 RADIO ONLINE: Connected to #{port} at #{speed} baud.\")\n        {:ok, %{uart: pid, port: port, active: true}}\n\n      {:error, reason} ->\n        Logger.warning(\"\u26a0\ufe0f RADIO OFFLINE: Could not open #{port} (#{inspect(reason)}). Entering Simulation Mode.\")\n        # We return :ok anyway, so the Supervisor doesn't crash the app\n        {:ok, %{uart: nil, port: port, active: false}}\n    end\n  end\n\n  # RECEIVING (RX): Incoming Bytes from EMAX\n  @impl true\n  def handle_info({:circuits_uart, _port, binary_data}, state) do\n    case Protocol.deserialize(binary_data) do\n      %SwarmBrain.Observation{} = obs ->\n        Logger.info(\"\u26a1\ufe0f RADIO RX: Received telegram ID:#{obs.id}\")\n        Pipeline.ingest_remote_signal(obs)\n      :error ->\n        :ok\n    end\n    {:noreply, state}\n  end\n\n  # SENDING (TX): Outgoing Observation\n  @impl true\n  def handle_cast({:tx, obs}, %{active: true} = state) do\n    packet = Protocol.serialize(obs)\n    UART.write(state.uart, packet)\n    {:noreply, state}\n  end\n\n  # SENDING (TX): Dummy Mode (Radio hardware missing)\n  def handle_cast({:tx, obs}, %{active: false} = state) do\n    Logger.debug(\"\ud83d\udc7b [SIMULATION] Radio TX would send: #{inspect(obs.id)}\")\n    # Here you could potentially write to a file to simulate transmission\n    {:noreply, state}\n  end\nend\n",
